turns.content: What brings you in today?

I’m really worried. I’ve had this thing happen four times now where my right hand goes completely numb for about an hour. It’s so strange—it just goes dead, like it’s not even attached to me. I keep thinking… could this be multiple sclerosis?
When did this start?

It started about a week and a half ago. The first time it happened, I was just sitting at my desk working, and suddenly my hand went numb. I tried to shake it off, but it didn’t come back for almost an hour. It’s been so alarming every time.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

There’s really no pain at all, which is almost scarier in a way. It’s just total numbness in my whole right hand—like from the wrist down to my fingertips. It feels completely dead, like it belongs to someone else. Sometimes I also get this weird dizziness, and I’ve been feeling more exhausted than usual, but that might just be from the worry.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills at all. My temperature's been normal as far as I know. It's just the numbness, the occasional dizziness, and this heavy fatigue that's been hanging over me.
What medications are you currently taking?
I'm not taking any medications at all. No prescriptions, no over-the-counter stuff either. That's partly why I'm so scared—there's nothing that could be causing this.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don’t have any chronic conditions. I’m generally healthy—never been diagnosed with anything like that. That’s why this numbness feels so out of the blue and terrifying.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I’m not allergic to any medications. I’ve never had a reaction to anything before.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Honestly, I haven't tried anything yet. Every time it happens, I just kind of freeze and wait for it to pass. I guess I've been too scared to do anything—I just keep hoping it won't come back, but it does.
Does anything make it better or worse?

It seems to happen more when I’m really stressed or exhausted from work. I’m a graphic designer, so I use my hands all day, and I’ve noticed the numbness tends to come on during busy deadlines. Rest doesn’t stop it once it starts—I just have to wait the hour out.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It’s been really hard. I can’t stop thinking about it, especially at work—I’m constantly checking my right hand, waiting for it to go numb again. It’s made me so anxious that I’m struggling to concentrate, and I keep imagining the worst. I just want to know what’s wrong with me.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don’t smoke at all, never have. I do have a drink occasionally—like a glass of wine with friends maybe once a week—but nothing regular or heavy.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No, no one in my family has ever had anything like this. That’s actually another reason I’m so scared—there’s no history of MS or any neurological issues. So this just feels completely out of nowhere.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, never. This is completely new to me. Before two weeks ago, I never had any numbness or weird symptoms like this. That's why it’s so frightening—it feels like my body is doing something it’s never done, and I have no idea why.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I’d say it’s only about a 1 or 2—the numbness doesn’t hurt, but it’s unsettling. Emotionally, though, I’m at a 9 or 10. I’m terrified this is something serious like MS, and I can’t stop thinking about it. It’s consuming me.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I think that's everything, but… I just want to say that sometimes before the numbness starts, I get this wave of dizziness, like the room tilts for a second. And I keep worrying that if it’s MS, it might get worse before we catch it. Please… I just need to know what’s going on.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Honestly, not well. I keep Googling symptoms late at night, which only makes me more panicked. I’ve tried telling myself it’s nothing, but every time my hand goes numb again, that voice gets quieter. I just feel so helpless waiting for the next episode.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I haven’t been coping well at all. I barely sleep because my mind races, and at work I keep zoning out, staring at my hand. A part of me keeps hoping it’s just stress or a pinched nerve, but the other part—the louder part—is convinced it’s something terrible. I just want an answer so I can stop spiraling.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I’m sorry—I know I keep saying the same things, but it’s just… I’m really scared. I feel like I’m stuck in this loop of worry, and every day without an answer makes it worse. I just need some clarity so I can breathe again.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I think I’ve said this a few times now, and I’m sorry if I’m repeating myself—it’s just that this fear is all I can think about. I guess the truth is I’m not coping at all. I just want to know what’s next—what tests can we do to figure this out?
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I’m sorry—I realize I’ve been saying the same thing over and over. It’s just all I can focus on. I guess the honest answer is that I’m not really coping; I’m just showing up here hoping you can help me find a way through this. That’s why I came today.
